headlines.title: Police officer was violent rapist who attacked women over 14 years, BBC reveals
headlines.description: The BBC can reveal that detectives have so far uncovered nine victims of PC Alan Greer but many more are anticipated.

headlines.title: BBC visits Iran school where strike killed 120 children
headlines.description: Nearly five months on, grief is still raw as a teacher and a mother recall the horror of the attack in Minab.

headlines.title: Friend of Manchester synagogue attacker jailed
headlines.description: Mohammad Bashir, 31, drove Jihad Al-Shamie on a trip to conduct hostile reconnaissance at a military base.

headlines.title: UK millionaires ask Andy Burnham to tax them more
headlines.description: Former footballer Gary Lineker and producer Brian Eno write to the new PM telling him "we can afford it".

headlines.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
headlines.description: The cost of parking closest to the terminal has hit £10 at two of the UK's airports.

headlines.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
headlines.description: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.

headlines.title: Houthis claim attack on oil tankers as US launches more strikes on Iran
headlines.description: The Houthi attacks would be the first in the Red Sea since the Iran-backed group announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia.

headlines.title: Glasgow set to welcome the world for scaled back Commonwealth Games
headlines.description: Athletes from 74 countries and territories will compete for 215 gold medals over 10 days of competition.

headlines.title: Thousands evacuated in France fires
headlines.description: A third firefighter has died in Europe while tackling ongoing wildfires across the continent.

headlines.title: Rain to return this weekend as parts of UK on brink of drought
headlines.description: Following weeks of mainly dry weather, we can welcome some rain this weekend, but it is not going to be that much, as Chris Fawkes explains.

headlines.title: Why the UK is dithering over what to do about e-scooters
headlines.description: Why is it taking so long for the government to regulate them, and what are the consequences of delay?

headlines.title: Is 2026 the Commonwealth Games' last dance or a future-proofing revamp?
headlines.description: It's been going for 96 years - but many doubted the 2026 Commonwealth Games, which open in Glasgow on Thursday, would happen. So what could the future hold for the event?

headlines.title: Andy Burnham's plan to collaborate with social media creators
headlines.description: The new prime minister plans to work with influencers and social media creators to get his message out.

headlines.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
headlines.description: Herders and experts tell the BBC how Africa's camels are suffering because of extreme heat.

headlines.title: Protest fit checks and tear gas reviews: How online humour is sustaining India's cockroach protesters
headlines.description: For 'cockroach' protesters, reels and memes became a way of documenting fear, making sense of violence and refusing to let it have the last word.

headlines.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
headlines.description: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

headlines.title: Hosepipe ban in force for millions of households - is your area affected?
headlines.description: More than eight million households have been placed under hosepipe bans - which has raised questions about how effectively water resources are being managed.

headlines.title: Secret court documents reveal potential scale of illegal sewage spilling
headlines.description: The new analysis from a prominent water campaigner emerged after secret court documents were leaked.

headlines.title: Seinfeld actor apologises to reality star for 'inappropriate' sketch when she was 17
headlines.description: Jason Alexander said he "truly regrets" the Funny Or Die sketch he appeared in with Courtney Stodden.

headlines.title: Former River City actor Iain Robertson jailed for nine years for rape
headlines.description: The 45-year-old, played Stevie O'Hara in the BBC soap, was convicted of five offences against the women between 2004 and 2019.

headlines.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
headlines.description: Fampridine boosts nerve signals to make it easier to walk, with up to 5,000 eligible.

headlines.title: Fatboy Slim helps to save 'lifeblood' music venue
headlines.description: The DJ is among investors including Andy Burnham helping to buy the Brighton home of The Pipeline.

headlines.title: Ann Widdecombe public memorial to be held later this year, family says
headlines.description: It will follow a private funeral for family and close friends, which will be held in the coming weeks.

headlines.title: Animal lover run over by own car while saving cat on hill
headlines.description: Jacqueline Davies was able to dial 999 after the incident, but died in hospital the following day.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Global Story: Is the Iran war back on?
headlines.description: The White House has asked Congress for billions more dollars in funding for the Iran war

headlines.title: Who is the new PM?
headlines.description: From Manchester mayor to prime minister; who is he and what does stand for?

headlines.title: Christopher Nolan's epic biopic starring Cillian Murphy
headlines.description: The life of J Robert Oppenheimer, the physicist known as the 'father of the atomic bomb'

headlines.title: No men's Ashes Test in north of England - full 2027 schedule
headlines.description: Neither Old Trafford nor Headingley will host a men's Ashes Test between England and Australia next summer.

headlines.title: Wolves training cancelled after Arokodare refuses to leave pitch
headlines.description: Wolves striker Tolu Arokodare forces the cancellation of a training session as he refuses to leave the pitch.

headlines.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
headlines.description: Major League Soccer says it is investigating allegations of tampering by Inter Miami after the club signed Brazil midfielder Casemiro.

headlines.title: 'Even the CEO doesn't know' - Kaymer on LIV's future
headlines.description: Martin Kaymer says it is "highly unlikely" LIV Golf's 2026 team championship will take place as he expresses doubt over the circuit's future.

headlines.title: Stormzy, two waste paper bins and a hotbed of rebellion
headlines.description: Derived from basketball and developed by teenagers in late-Victorian England, netball is sport's great misunderstood "girly game". It could be heading to the Olympics in 2032.

headlines.title: One month, two record transfers - why English midfielders are in demand
headlines.description: Elliot Anderson and Morgan Rogers both sold for record transfer fees, so why are English midfielders so in demand?

headlines.title: Ex-UFC champion Andrade working as delivery driver
headlines.description:
Jessica Andrade says she will do "whatever it takes" to earn a living as she recovers from injury.

has been working as a delivery driver to earn extra money while she is recovering from injury.
